metadata.name: descriptastorus
metadata.summary: Descriptor creation, storage and molecular file indexing

functions.name: descriptastorus.utils.raw_to_libsvm
documentation:
Write a raw store to libsvm format

```
param ofile

file like object to write to

param store

the raw store

param full_first_header

Write the full first line regardless of sparseness
```

methods.name: descriptastorus.DescriptaStore.DescriptaStore.__init__
documentation:
dbdir -> opens a descriptor storage

```pycon
>>> store = DescriptaStore(db)
>>> len(store)
```

\# access the options used to create this store
\#  (this is optional and may not exist)
\>>> store.options
...

Iterate through molecule data (\[moldata, \<optional name>\], descriptors)
\>>> for moldata, descriptors in store:
\>>>     pass

Iterate through only the descriptors
\>>> for i,prop in enumerate(store.descriptors()):
\>>>    pass

If name indexed:
\>>> row = store.lookupName("ZWIMER-03065")

If inchi key index:
Since inchi keys may collide, this can return multiple indices
\>>>  rows = store.lookupInchiKey("BCWYEXBNOWJQJV-UHFFFAOYSA-N")

methods.name: descriptastorus.DescriptaStore.DescriptaStore.getDescriptorCalculator
documentation:
Returns the descriptor calculator (if possible) for the store
In general this requires the same run-time environment as the 
storage, so this might not be possible

methods.name: descriptastorus.DescriptaStore.DescriptaStore.getDescriptorNames
documentation:
keepCalculatedFlags=False -> return the descriptor names for the store
if keepCalculatedFlags is True return the boolean flags that indicate
if results were calculated for the descriptor subset.

methods.name: descriptastorus.DescriptaStore.DescriptaStore.getDescriptors
documentation:
index, keepCalculatedFlags=False -> return the descriptors at index
if keepCalculatedFlags is True return the boolean flags that indicate
if results were calculated for the descriptor subset.

methods.name: descriptastorus.DescriptaStore.DescriptaStore.getDescriptorsAsDict
documentation: index -> return the descriptors as an index

methods.name: descriptastorus.DescriptaStore.DescriptaStore.descriptors
documentation: Returns the raw storage for the descriptors

methods.name: descriptastorus.DescriptaStore.DescriptaStore.molIndex
documentation: Returns the mol index

functions.name: descriptastorus.MolFileIndex.MakeSmilesIndex
documentation:
Make smiles index -> index a smiles file for random access
filename: filename to index
dbdir: name of the index store
hasHeader: do we have a header
smilesColumn: column for the smiles string
nameColumn: column for the name string
sep: seperator for the file i.e. '  '
reIndex: reIndex the existing file
otherwise Copies file over to index

functions.name: descriptastorus.MolFileIndex.MakeSDFIndex
documentation: Make smiles index -> index a smiles file for random access
, os.path.basename(filename), smilesColumn,
nameColumn=nameColumn, hasHeader=hasHeader,
sep=sep)

classes.name: descriptastorus.MolFileIndex.MolFileIndex

methods.name: descriptastorus.MolFileIndex.MolFileIndex.__init__
documentation:
Fast random access to a smiles file by row index
indexDirectory = directory of the molfile index

Example
-----------
See MakeSmilesIndex and MakeSDF Index to make indexed files

idx -> gets the data at row idx
return a list if the data is a smiles like file
returns a string buffer otherwise

Index for a molecule file to provide random access to the internal molecules.

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.MakeGenerator
documentation:
Make a descriptor generator by combining multiple generators

```
param generator_names

list of available generator names

result

DescriptorGenerator
```

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.create_descriptors
documentation:
pyjanitor style function for using the descriptor generator

Convert a column of smiles strings or RDKIT Mol objects into Descriptors.
Returns a new dataframe without any of the original data. This is
intentional, as Descriptors are usually high-dimensional
features.

This method does not mutate the original DataFrame.

```python import pandas as pd import descriptastorus.descriptors df = pd.DataFrame(...) # For "counts" kind descriptors = descriptastorus.descriptors.create_descriptors(     mols_column_name='smiles', generator_names=["Morgan3Count"])

```

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.molFromSmiles
documentation: Prepare a smiles to a molecule

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.molFromMol
documentation: Do any internal preperation required from a user-mol

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.GetColumns
documentation: Returns [(name, numpy.dtype), ...] for all columns being computed

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.calculateMol
documentation: Override me for the actual calculation

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.processMol
documentation:
rdmol, smiles -> result
generate descriptors from a smiles string using the specified
properties.  

Takes the molecule as-is.  Calling this directly requires the User
to properly prepare the molecule

The first value returned is always True to indicate that the
descriptors have actually been set in the store

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.processMols
documentation:
mols, smiles -> results
Process the molecules.  Note that smiles
may not actually be smiles strings, but molblocks as well
this is used for error reporting

if internalParsing is False, takes the molecules as-is.  Otherwise
the molecule was prepared by the DescriptorGenerator by calling the appropriate
translation function (i.e. molFromSmiles) (e.g. used for consistently
ordering input for MoKa descriptors)  

Calling this directly requires the User to properly prepare the molecules if necessary

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.process
documentation:
smiles string -> descriptors
returns None for invalid smiles strings

generate descriptors from a smiles string using the specified
properties.  

Default is to return morgan3 folded counts clipped to 255 and
use rdkit 2D properties.

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.processSmiles
documentation:
smiles -> descriptors
Process many smiles string and generate the descriptors

class_variables.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.DescriptorGenerator.MAX_CACHE
documentation: set to 0 to disable caching

methods.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.DescriptorGenerator.Container.__init__
documentation: Note containers do NOT get stored in the registry

modules.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.QED
documentation:
$Id: QED.py 11163 2013-10-07 09:43:54Z landrgr1 $

QED stands for quantitative estimation of drug-likeness and the concept was for the first time introduced by Richard Bickerton and 
coworkers [1]. The empirical rationale of the QED measure reflects the underlying distribution of molecular properties including 
molecular weight, logP, topological polar surface area, number of hydrogen bond donors and acceptors, the number of aromatic rings 
and rotatable bonds, and the presence of unwanted chemical functionalities.
The QED results as generated by the RDKit-based implementation of Biscu-it(tm) are not completely identical to those from the original 
publication [1]. These differences are a consequence of differences within the underlying calculated property calculators used in 
both methods. For example, discrepancies can be noted in the results from the logP calculations, nevertheless despite the fact that 
both approaches (Pipeline Pilot in the original publication and RDKit in our Biscu-it(tm) implementation) mention to use the Wildmann 
and Crippen methodology for the calculation of their logP-values [2]. However, the differences in the resulting QED-values are very 
small and are not compromising the usefullness of using Qed in your daily research.

[1] Bickerton, G.R.; Paolini, G.V.; Besnard, J.; Muresan, S.; Hopkins, A.L. (2012) 'Quantifying the chemical beauty of drugs', 
    Nature Chemistry, 4, 90-98 [http://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nchem.1243]
    
History:
  2012-04 Adapted to internal RDkit implementation
  2013-05 moved to rdkit.Chem.QED
Copyright (c) 2013-2017, Novartis Institutes for BioMedical Research Inc.
All rights reserved.
Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.QED.ads
documentation: ADS function

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.QED.properties
documentation: Calculates the properties that are required to calculate the QED descriptor.

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.QED.qed
documentation:
Calculate the weighted sum of ADS mapped properties

some examples from the QED paper, reference values from Peter G's original implementation
\>>> m = Chem.MolFromSmiles('N=C(CCSCc1csc(N=C(N)N)n1)NS(N)(=O)=O')
\>>> qed(m)
0.241...
\>>> m = Chem.MolFromSmiles('CNC(=NCCSCc1nc\[nH\]c1C)NC#N')
\>>> qed(m)
0.217...
\>>> m = Chem.MolFromSmiles('CCCCCNC(=N)NN=Cc1c\[nH\]c2ccc(CO)cc12')
\>>> qed(m)
0.212...
\>>> asdf

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.QED.weights_max
documentation: Calculates the QED descriptor using maximal descriptor weights.

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.QED.weights_mean
documentation: Calculates the QED descriptor using average descriptor weights.

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.QED.weights_none
documentation: Calculates the QED descriptor using unit weights.

functions.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.QED.default
documentation: Calculates the QED descriptor using average descriptor weights.

documentation: doctest boilerplate

classes.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.rdNormalizedDescriptors.RDKit2DNormalized
documentation:
Distribution normalized descriptors.
These are then converted to a CDF so for a given value v
the result is the percentage of the population under that value

classes.name: descriptastorus.descriptors.rdNormalizedDescriptors.RDKit2DHistogramNormalized
documentation:
Histogrram normalized descriptors.
These are then converted to a CDF so for a given value v
the result is the percentage of the population under that value

These are WAY faster than the corresponding Distribution normalized 
descriptors

classes.name: descriptastorus.keyvalue.KeyValueAPI

methods.name: descriptastorus.keyvalue.KeyValueAPI.get_raw
documentation:
Get the value for a particular key (str or bytes
depending on the python version)

methods.name: descriptastorus.keyvalue.KeyValueAPI.set_raw
documentation:
Set the value for a particular key.  Values 
must be str or bytes depending on the python version
Simple API to wrap various key value stores

modules.name: descriptastorus.raw
documentation:
Raw storage of integer and floating point data.

This provides fast random access to indexed data.

r = RawStore(directory)
row = r.get(0)
row = r.get(100000)

Data can be updated using putRow when opened in append mode.

classes.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStoreIter
documentation: raw stores are little endian!

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.__init__
documentation:
Raw storage engine
directory = existing directory to read or prepare the raw storage
if N is None, an existing directory is created with N entries

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.appendBlankRows
documentation: Adds M blank rows to the store (must be opened in append mode)

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.append
documentation: Append one raw store to another

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.getDict
documentation: {colname:value, ...} Return the row at idx as a dictionary

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.get
documentation: Return the row at idx

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.getColByIdx
documentation: Return the data in the entire column (lazy generator)

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.getCol
documentation:
Return the column addressed by column_name
throws IndexError if the column doesn't exist.

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.putRow
documentation:
Put data row in to row idx.
Checks to see if the data in v is compatible with the column
formats.  Throws ValueError on failure.

methods.name: descriptastorus.raw.RawStore.write
documentation:
Writes row with no datatype checking to the end of the file.
Do not use with putRow/getRow
Rows must be written in the correct order
(strings datatypes are currently an issue)
